project.name: amrul-wi
project.description: Static wedding invitation website for Hani & Amrul, built as a single-page HTML site with jQuery, CSS animation plugins, and locally hosted media assets.

nodes.name: 01_TECHNICAL_BASELINE.md
nodes.summary: Establishes the static wedding-invitation technical baseline: stack (HTML/jQuery/plugins), asset flow without a build step, folder map, runtime smoke-test results, functional features to preserve, and audit scope limitations.
nodes.languageNotes: Anchors the cleanup audit with observed runtime behavior on desktop and mobile viewports.

nodes.name: 02_ASSET_INVENTORY.md
nodes.summary: Catalogs 191 local assets (33.01 MiB) across media, plugin, public/dist, and src with per-group reference tracing, duplicate status, confidence ratings, and inventory exclusion rules.

nodes.name: 03_BROKEN_AND_MISSING_ASSETS.md
nodes.summary: Documents confirmed broken and missing assets including Orn-64.png, incomplete lightGallery distribution, missing html2canvas source map, stale commented template script, and root-relative URL deployment risk.

nodes.name: 04_UNUSED_AND_DUPLICATE_FINDINGS.md
nodes.summary: Lists high-confidence unused asset, duplicate-hash, dead-code, and unused-dependency candidates with evidence, removal risk, and SHA-256 duplicate groups; no deletions performed by the audit.

nodes.name: 05_CDN_MIGRATION_ANALYSIS.md
nodes.summary: Evaluates each CDN and local library dependency with conservative migration recommendations, prioritizing KEEP_LOCAL for critical plugins and pinning/SRI hardening for unpinned third-party URLs.

nodes.name: 06_FINDINGS_REGISTER.md
nodes.summary: Consolidated findings register merging asset, dead-code, CDN security, and metadata issues with severity, evidence, user impact, risk, and confidence for each tracked finding ID.

nodes.name: 07_REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Defines seven isolated PR phases from smoke-test baseline through asset repair, dead-file removal, CDN hardening, optional build pipeline, and final validation with explicit scope and rollback rules.

nodes.name: 08_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md
nodes.summary: Executive verdict that bulk deletion and CDN migration are not ready; recommends safety-net PR, two targeted asset repairs, ordered quick wins, and lists unsafe areas requiring extra evidence before change.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything exclusion patterns file using gitignore syntax to skip directories and test files from codebase analysis.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything plugin settings disabling auto-update and setting English as the documentation output language.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Single-page wedding invitation entry point for Hani and Amrul featuring inline config (RSVP, music, layouts), Alsa template sections, CDN plugins, and embedded media references.
nodes.languageNotes: Monolithic HTML with inline @font-face blocks, window globals for RSVP/music/layout, and deferred jQuery plugin loading.

nodes.name: flexbin.css
nodes.summary: Flexbin masonry gallery stylesheet defining flex-wrap image grid layout with responsive breakpoints.

nodes.name: slick.css
nodes.summary: Slick carousel plugin base styles for slider track, list, arrows, and touch-friendly scrolling.

nodes.name: jquery.js
nodes.summary: Vendored jQuery v3.5.1 library providing DOM manipulation, events, and AJAX utilities for the site.
nodes.languageNotes: UMD-wrapped IIFE bundle; tree-sitter extraction yields no top-level named functions.

nodes.name: html2canvas-1.4.1.1749130377.js
nodes.summary: Vendored html2canvas 1.4.1 library that renders DOM elements to canvas for invitation screenshot export.
nodes.languageNotes: UMD factory bundle exposing global html2canvas; no extractable top-level function nodes.

nodes.name: universal.js
nodes.summary: Core application JavaScript with shared UI helpers, AJAX posting, form widgets, and document-ready event bindings for the wedding site.

nodes.name: openModal
nodes.summary: Opens a modal overlay, optionally clearing any existing modal first and appending new content.

nodes.name: closeModal
nodes.summary: Hides the primary modal, restores body scroll, and clears modal HTML content.

nodes.name: showAlert
nodes.summary: Displays a styled alert message with optional auto-dismiss after a delay.

nodes.name: postData
nodes.summary: Sends FormData via jQuery AJAX with loader, success/error callbacks, and upload progress hooks.

nodes.name: copyToClipboard
nodes.summary: Copies text to the clipboard using the legacy execCommand API with fallback handling.

nodes.name: getOffset
nodes.summary: Calculates cumulative offset coordinates of a DOM element relative to the document.

nodes.name: generate_tooltip
nodes.summary: Builds and positions a custom tooltip on hover with viewport-aware placement logic.

nodes.name: generate_chart_data
nodes.summary: Transforms raw chart input data into formatted series values for visualization.

nodes.name: init_selectize
nodes.summary: Initializes Selectize dropdown widgets on matching elements with shared options.

nodes.name: selectize_options
nodes.summary: Returns default Selectize configuration options for searchable dropdowns.

nodes.name: whatsapp_template_selection_options
nodes.summary: Builds Selectize option list for WhatsApp message template selection.

nodes.name: whatsapp_template_selection
nodes.summary: Handles WhatsApp template dropdown change events and updates related form state.

nodes.name: guest_group_selection_options
nodes.summary: Builds Selectize option list for guest group filtering and selection.

nodes.name: guest_group_selection
nodes.summary: Responds to guest group dropdown changes and triggers dependent UI updates.

nodes.name: main_event_selection_options
nodes.summary: Builds Selectize option list for main wedding event selection.

nodes.name: main_event_selection
nodes.summary: Handles main event dropdown changes and coordinates related form fields.

nodes.name: populateSelectOptions
nodes.summary: Populates a select element with options from a data array and refreshes Selectize.

nodes.name: counter
nodes.summary: Animates numeric counters with progress bar width updates and formatted display.

nodes.name: thousands
nodes.summary: Formats numbers with thousands separators for display in counters and stats.

nodes.name: textarea_height
nodes.summary: Auto-resizes textarea height to fit content on keyup and focus events.

nodes.name: goto_calculator
nodes.summary: Navigates to a calculator page with query parameters derived from link attributes.

nodes.name: init_tab
nodes.summary: Activates the default tab on page load by triggering click on marked tab nav items.

nodes.name: tab_content_toggle
nodes.summary: Switches active tab state and shows/hides associated tab content panels.

nodes.name: animateCSS
nodes.summary: Applies Animate.css classes to an element and removes them after animation ends.

nodes.name: getViewport
nodes.summary: Returns current viewport width and height from window dimensions.

nodes.name: getMobileOperatingSystem
nodes.summary: Detects mobile OS (iOS, Android, Windows Phone) from user agent string.

nodes.name: downloadFile
nodes.summary: Triggers a file download by creating a temporary anchor element with blob URL.

nodes.name: checkNetwork
nodes.summary: Monitors online/offline status and shows alerts when network connectivity changes.

nodes.name: universalSearch
nodes.summary: Filters DOM elements by matching search query text against element content.

nodes.name: accordion_toogle
nodes.summary: Toggles accordion panel expand/collapse with slide animations within a wrapper.

nodes.name: 4964c82c.js
nodes.summary: Hash-named application JavaScript bundle referenced from index.html; initializes AOS, lightGallery, Slick, Selectize, modal-video, Video.js, tsParticles, forms/AJAX, and language selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Compiled/minified output with no build provenance in-repo.

nodes.name: ca7f5103.css
nodes.summary: Hash-named theme CSS bundle linked from index.html; includes ALSA template frame/background custom properties and typography references.

nodes.name: lightgallery.css
nodes.summary: lightGallery v1.4.1-beta.0 stylesheet declaring fonts, loading indicator, and video-provider graphics via relative URLs; distribution missing dist/fonts and dist/img assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Referenced CSS exists but companion font/image assets are missing from the checked-in distribution.

layers.name: Entry & Markup
layers.description: Single-page HTML shell for the Hani & Amrul wedding invitation, wiring script tags, stylesheet links, inline RSVP config, and all section markup.

layers.name: Application Logic
layers.description: Site-specific JavaScript that drives RSVP forms, AJAX posting, UI helpers, plugin initialization, and language selection for the wedding invitation.

layers.name: Vendor Libraries
layers.description: Third-party JavaScript libraries vendored locally—jQuery for DOM/events and html2canvas for invitation screenshot export.

layers.name: Styles & Plugins
layers.description: Compiled theme CSS bundle plus plugin stylesheets for flexbin gallery grid, Slick carousel, and lightGallery lightbox presentation.

layers.name: Media Assets
layers.description: Locally hosted wedding photos, gallery WebP variants, and Butler/Bebas Neue font files referenced by index.html and compiled CSS.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Eight-part codebase cleanup audit documenting asset inventory, broken references, CDN migration analysis, findings register, and remediation plan.

layers.name: Tooling & Config
layers.description: Understand-Anything plugin configuration and ignore patterns used for codebase analysis tooling, not runtime site behavior.

tour.title: Wedding Invitation Entry Point
tour.description: Start with index.html, the single-page entry point for Hani and Amrul's wedding invitation. This file defines the full guest experience in one document: inline configuration for RSVP, music, and layout options, Alsa template sections (hero, couple, gallery, events), and script or stylesheet tags that wire up every runtime dependency. Understanding its structure gives you a map of the entire site before diving into individual assets.
tour.languageLesson: Static sites often use a single HTML file as both the page structure and the configuration surface — inline JavaScript objects or data attributes hold guest-facing settings without a separate build step.

tour.title: Compiled Theme Bundles
tour.description: Building on the script and link tags in index.html, the public/dist folder holds hash-named bundles that power the visual theme and plugin initialization. The CSS bundle (ca7f5103.css) carries Alsa template custom properties, typography rules, and frame backgrounds, while the JavaScript bundle (4964c82c.js) boots AOS scroll animations, lightGallery, Slick carousels, Selectize dropdowns, modal-video, Video.js, tsParticles, form AJAX, and language switching. These pre-built artifacts mean the site runs without a local compile step.

tour.title: Core Application Logic
tour.description: src/universal.js is the hand-written application layer that sits alongside the compiled bundle. It provides shared UI helpers — modal open/close, alert toasts, AJAX postData, clipboard copy, Selectize form widgets, tab toggles, and document-ready event bindings — that the wedding forms and interactive sections rely on. With the highest fan-out in the codebase, this file is the central hub connecting guest-facing behavior to jQuery and the DOM.
tour.languageLesson: jQuery's $(document).ready() pattern defers initialization until the DOM is parsed, letting universal.js safely attach event handlers and enhance form controls before the guest interacts with the page.

tour.title: jQuery Foundation
tour.description: The site vendors jQuery 3.5.1 locally in src/jquery.js rather than loading it from a CDN. universal.js depends on jQuery for DOM selection, event binding, and AJAX — the same primitives the compiled bundle uses for plugin initialization. Keeping jQuery local ensures the invitation works offline or on slow networks, which matters for guests opening the link at a venue with poor connectivity.

tour.title: Gallery Plugin Styles
tour.description: Three plugin stylesheets in the plugin/ directory shape the photo and carousel experiences referenced from index.html. flexbin.css lays out the masonry-style gallery grid, slick.css styles the carousel track and navigation arrows, and lightgallery.css provides lightbox overlay, loading indicators, and video-provider graphics. Together they define how wedding photos are displayed, scrolled, and enlarged — note that the lightGallery distribution is incomplete (missing dist/fonts and dist/img assets documented in the audit).

tour.title: Screenshot Export Library
tour.description: src/js/html2canvas-1.4.1.1749130377.js is a vendored copy of html2canvas that renders DOM elements onto a canvas, enabling guests to save or share a screenshot of the invitation. This library is loaded separately from the main bundle and supports the share-or-download feature without requiring server-side rendering. The audit notes its source map is missing, which affects debugging but not guest-facing functionality.
tour.languageLesson: html2canvas walks the DOM tree, computes styles, and paints each element onto an HTML5 canvas — a client-side alternative to server-side headless browser screenshot APIs.

tour.title: Custom Typography
tour.description: The invitation's elegant look depends on locally hosted OpenType fonts in media/external/. Bebas Neue Regular provides bold display headings, while the Butler family (Regular, Light, and Bold) supplies the serif body and accent typography defined via @font-face rules in the compiled CSS bundle. Self-hosting fonts avoids CDN latency and keeps the typographic identity consistent even when third-party services are unreachable.
tour.languageLesson: @font-face in CSS declares custom fonts with src pointing to local files; browsers download only the weights actually referenced in stylesheets, so unused font files become cleanup candidates.

tour.title: Wedding Photo Gallery
tour.description: The flexbin gallery section in index.html references pairs of WebP images for each wedding photo: a _1.webp thumbnail for the responsive grid and a full-resolution .webp opened in the lightGallery lightbox. These assets live under media/external/ with imgbb-sourced filenames. The thumbnail-plus-full pattern keeps initial page load fast while preserving high-quality enlargement when a guest taps a photo.

tour.title: Couple Section Portraits
tour.description: Beyond the event gallery, the couple section displays portrait WebP images of each partner with the same thumbnail-and-lightbox pairing used elsewhere. These responsive portraits are among the most visible assets on the page and are directly referenced from index.html. They illustrate how media/external/ serves both decorative hero content and interactive lightGallery targets throughout the single-page layout.

tour.title: Codebase Audit Documentation
tour.description: The docs/codebase-cleanup-audit/ folder documents a systematic review of this static site. Start with 01_TECHNICAL_BASELINE.md for the stack overview, folder map, and features to preserve, then read 08_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md for the verdict on safe cleanup actions. These docs explain known broken assets, unused files, CDN hardening recommendations, and a phased remediation plan — essential context if you plan to modify or deploy the site.
